sentences.text: Morning!
Hi there!
I get it.
How are you all doing?
Good!
Good, how are you?
Pretty good.
It's always, just a great day to be back from a long weekend.
Do you all get up to anything fun?
Nothing really.
Kind of low-key for me.
It was a good weekend to hang out with my family, so it was… it was nice.
That's nice.
We did, we did Topgolf and cleaned the garage out yesterday, so…
Dead!
Pretty low-key on this end as well.
And that's…
I don't know.
Yeah.
Well, good to see you both.
I know we don't get to connect that much over the course of the year, so, nice to see you both again, and wanted to just kick off to, see how things are going.

Make sure you're still happy with the IQ at the service level that we're at, and then I can walk you through Just some new product lines that we have to see if there's interest there, and otherwise we can… Do a pretty flat, renewal for you all.
Awesome.

Would you be able to, Matthew, can you, when you send the renewal to us, can you show, like, what we're currently paying right now by each property, and then same with the renewal rate by each property, so not, like, total portfolio cost, but broken out with what's actually getting billed to each site?
Yep, I think I should be able to… I will be able to pull out of the back end just each property and their unit count, and then I think we're, 45 cents a unit, against each property.
So, I'm gonna just run that in Excel and get that over to you.
Okay.
Awesome.
But things are going well, we're happy, we want to renew.
Yep.
Awesome.
Any planned growth for NatDev this year?
You all looking at, expanding the portfolio, or, how has business been in general?
Always looking to expand the portfolio.
Always.
I mean, we don't have anything that we, like, know of today.
I mean, we do have some sites that are in construction, but that probably won't be until 2027 that we open.
But always looking to acquire, so that is definitely a possibility for, next year.
Okay, and, in terms of, like, your acquisition process, currently do you have access to, like, a CoStar or Market Analytics?
What softwares are you using for that right now?
co-star.
Oh, sorry.
And that would be, like, our asset team, that… that would be using CoStar.
And then on our side, like, we'll dig in a little bit in ApartmentIQ and use, some reports from there.
So we'll… we'll use both.
Okay, great.
Just because, like, as we're… so I'll walk through the product now, but two major product releases for, IQ… In the last couple of quarters.
One is that we did announce a revenue management software.
So… I'm not sure what you all are using there, but, functionally, the revenue management software, Daylight, works like the incumbents in terms of how the pricing algorithms are structured.
But it has a few additional components to it, one of which is, what we call explainable AI.
So, for every pricing suggestion that we're giving you within the tool, we're giving you the reasoning behind that.
So, kind of like.
drawn out, summarized for you, on each individual unit and each individual floor plan, why the pricing suggestion is being made.

So… It's gotten pretty popular pretty fast, powered by all public data, so obviously with the, conversation in the space around price-fixing algorithms and private data, some popularity there, and then… The amount of information that's going into the tool is about 16 million leases of data.
And so… what we found is that the suggestions that are being drawn out from that are more in line with what the revenue management teams would be looking for.
So… Won't go into that today, but just want to let you know that that exists.

The second tool is a tool called, Explore, and we'll go into that a little bit today, and that, again, would be for that, acquisitions or asset management team that's looking at, growing the portfolio, functionally, a replacement of CoStar.
So, that released, last quarter.

And I'll just talk through that quickly so you have an overview of what that looks like, and then if there's interest there, as we're talking renewal, if it's something we want to look at, I can provide pricing for that as well.
Awesome.
Cool.
Let me share my screen… And take a sip of coffee.
Before you get into it, can I just ask a quick question?
So, on this new platform that potentially could replace CoStar, is this a one-time cost that, like, we could charge to our asset team, or is this… would this add on to, like, our unit count cost?
No, it's separate.
So, it's, like CoStar, it's license-based.

It's a little different in that there's not, geographical restrictions to it, but Same concept, it's like a per-seat cost, and then, that is a monthly cost, but it's… we're generally contracting it separate, obviously, for Market Survey, because that's being billed back to, like, the property, so it doesn't really make sense.
Perfect.
Yep.
Awesome.
So… there is now 5 areas for navigation within IQ, so my properties, this is… What you all are accessing on a weekly basis, your existing portfolio.

Second component here is research, so this is generally to support the underwriting process for, those acquisitions teams, development teams, or client services if they're targeting change of management.
Are you familiar with this section of the tool, like the research section?
I think so, we've looked at this before.
Okay, yeah, functionally, like, the same set of reports that you're getting in the My Properties section of the tool, it's just for your non-owned, non-managed.

So, way that it works, you type a property name, and it's gonna auto-generate the most, comp set with the most, like, product around that property, just using, like, our, scoring algorithm of each property.
Explore, this should look, brand new, so let's just say I take Dallas as an example.
Functionally, what you have here, is a few new elements.
So you've got the, inventory that exists in ApartmentIQ that's being collected the exact same way.
So, scrapers are going out nightly from 10 p.
m.
Eastern until 5 a.
m.
Eastern the following morning, updating all the data.
At each piece of existing inventory within the tool.

On top of that, we've added in, government databases and building permit supply within the tool, and then we've partnered with Real Capital Analytics, to get more background on that supply, things like transaction cost, and delivery date, and size of that, that project.
So, existing inventory within a market, and then new supply within a market.
We've also added in ACS census data and things like U.
S.
Bureau of Labor data, to shore up some demographic information in each market, sub-market as well.
So, this functions a lot like most of the, user interface within IQ, in that there's a filtering function up top.
And then in this case, you can search any geographical boundary, so MSA all the way down to, individual zip code or neighborhood.

And then you also have some freehand tools over here, so as you're looking at certain areas, all the data is going to reflect what you have selected in terms of boundary, as well as what you have filtered down to.
So, filter system here, like, for that asset team, right, this is gonna look pretty familiar.
Things like property class.
We do have management and ownership filters as well.
Construction status, and your typical, like, performance factors, they're looking for certain occupancies.
Certain unit sizes at property, certain class of properties, they can kind of filter the inventory down to that.
Left-hand side here is going to be historical rent and performance.
As well as a new forecasting module.
So, we recently brought over, Mike Taylor, who led CoStar's forecasting for the last 10 years, enabled him with our data and technology.
This is currently live in the top 150 MSAs.
And we'll continue to kind of focus down, so same way up here that you can go, like, MSA, county zip code.
Eventually, this forecasting will get more granular to, granular to those areas.
So, 5-year forecasting right now, on rent.
Current state of the market, so outside of your direct competitive set, What are asking rents?
What are concession values?
How many units are available on market?
Down here, you have that supply module, so you can actually click in.
See when units are delivery and their distribution size, and then get a summary of those projects as well.
And then, again, demographic information.
For whichever area you have selected.
So this is down to the individual census tract.
You can literally boundary, like, around a 2 or 3 block radius, and all of this data is going to reflect that.
So, standard factors here, population, education, income, and employment.
The partnership with RCA is really going to shore up some of this data.
So, for instance, instead of, employment by occupation top 10, we'll have this data, but we'll also have things like top employers in that market.
Okay.
Can we look at this for Boston real quick?
Absolutely.
Do you want to do, like, the MSA, or do you want to get down to, like, city level, or any…
Let's 10.
MSA?
Cool.
Not bad compared to most MSAs.
I don't know if it feels that way, but just from the initial…
Oh, it's… Boston's usually a pretty strong market.
Yeah, so I think you can do, like, you know, this is, this is really cool, I might have to type it out.
It's a national…
That's just gonna be your inventory, and then your performance.
Your 30-day snapshot.
And then, each of your properties here.
Huh?
Is there anything in particular you want to see in Boston?
No, I was just more curious,
Yeah, it keeps scrolling a little bit.
Let me clear that filter off, one sec.
Is, demographic information most… of most interest?
Yeah, I was just… I was just curious.
Yeah.
You can do, like…
Looking at demographics, I'm just, where it says, like, occupation, does it include students?
I actually don't know, that's a great question.
I don't think that it would.
Okay.
What you do have, though, is this education module in here as well.
So this is college graduates year over year here.
So it's like, how many college graduates this year?
And then you have this population as well, so if you're looking at, like, census groups, you've got your… here's your, I guess… You know, a portion of these would be your students.
Awesome.
I think this is really cool.
Do you, mind just shooting us some pricing?
If it's something that our asset team's interested in, I could have them reach out to you to do, like, a specific demo for them on it, but they'll probably just be interested in the pricing per seat.
Yeah, absolutely.
I'll quickly show you this, just because it's pretty relevant to, like, IQ as a whole.
As a part of this, there's these new property pages within Explore.
And so, you'll see some new information here, like, this is actually one of your properties, the last sales price, who the ownership group is, management company.
And then, your performance.
I'm a Slate Co-Star.
What is that?
This looks like CoStar Report.
Right, yeah, exactly.
So this is like, hey, this is awesome for you, this is your property, and then what we do is we're auto-generating a set of 10 comps with our data.
So you're outperforming the comps here, by $97 on gross rents of the subject property.
and then overperforming the market by about $400 at that property.
So this is for… Rents, but you also have it for exposure and occupancy.
Concession value, and then leasing activity.
And then, much like within IQ, where you can drill into any floor plan or the trend, you have that information right here as well.
Here's an estimated rent roll, so given that we have 5 years of historical data, we're able to estimate this for every property.
Here's your comp set.
Directly out of here, that research module that we looked at, you can now generate a report.
So for that underwriting support that we're providing, you can look high level at a market, and then drill directly into it, and then create a report directly out of there.
So should, from, like, a user, experience, be a really smooth, really smooth process.
One last thing I'll show you, I know you're primarily focused East Coast and Boston, but there's this market comparison tool as well.
So this function is like a marketer performance analytics with RealPage, pretty similar tool to that.
Where, all your most important data factors in terms of, like, property performance.
All of your most important data factors for, Market opportunity.
So, how many units are sitting on market, what's been delivered, what are transaction sizes, what are the type of builds that are happening there?
And then all of your demographic information.
And I'm looking at the last 12 months of this, which you can track essentially, like, any time frame you want, and we're gonna give you trend data on each of these,
Right now, I've got MSAs, but you can look at counties and cities as well.
So if you just want to look Massachusetts cities and see where is a, like, ripe opportunity to potentially develop or acquire properties.
Higher level overview for you.
That you can then directly click into, and it's gonna take you back to that page we were just at.
Awesome.

Yeah, so the eventual idea here is, like, instead of having 10 different logins to different data providers, you have your functional coastal replacement, your underwriting help, your operational side of things, all under one login.

But I will send you the pricing that you asked for and a summary of this, and then, in terms of the market survey, it's generally between, like, a 3% and 5%, so I'll just run it through finance and get that over to you.
Awesome.
Great.
Cool.
That's really great.
Awesome.
Well, it is, Tuesday after a long weekend, so we each get 10 minutes back, which sounds… sounds good.
Thank you, sorry, I did just have one quick question.
So, would you just mind repeating what our current rate is?
45 cents a unit.
Okay, and it's… you… it's roughly gonna be, like, a.
I'd say, I guess…
because, at your unit count, it would generally be 85 cents a unit, like, market rate, but you all got in so early, I think in, like, 2021, that it's just… you win.
You have grandfathered rates.
So, but generally, like, when you have that bracket with finance, we'll say, we're going top-end, because it's so far off from market rates.
So, I'd anticipate 5%, but I'll confirm that and send that over.
Awesome.
Thanks so much.
Great seeing you both.
Thank you.
Okay, bye-bye.
